title: Pinterest's AI-Powered Growth Strategy
text: Pinterest has undergone a significant transformation, evolving into what CEO Bill Ready describes as an AI-powered visual-first shopping assistant. The company's latest earnings report reveals how this strategic pivot is driving measurable growth. Third-quarter revenue reached $1.049 billion, up 17% year-over-year, with monthly active users climbing 12% to 600 million. The platform processed 80 billion monthly queries—a remarkable 44% increase from the previous year. This surge in search volume reflects users' growing engagement with Pinterest's visual discovery capabilities. Artificial intelligence now operates throughout Pinterest's ecosystem. The company launched a multimodal search tool combining text and image inputs, alongside a conversational Pinterest Assistant that transforms open-ended prompts into curated visual results. Ready emphasized that the platform aims to anticipate what users will love next, distinguishing itself from traditional search engines by proactively surfacing inspiration. The integration of commerce features shows promise, with outbound clicks to advertisers rising 40% in Q3. Users with Amazon-linked accounts can now move directly from Pinterest pins to checkout in a near one-click process, demonstrating what the company calls agentic commerce. Pinterest projects Q4 revenue between $1.313-$1.338 billion, representing 14-16% growth.
